briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposed AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse, raising serious questions about fintech deposit protection.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deepening ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments industry and what it means for consumers and fintechs alike.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit cards into increasingly niche and unconventional territory, from crypto rewards to hyper-targeted perks for specific communities.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Wealthsimple gets regulatory approval to enter prediction markets](https://betakit.com/wealthsimple-gets-regulatory-approval-to-enter-prediction-markets/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech giant Wealthsimple has received regulatory approval to offer prediction markets, marking a significant expansion of its product suite beyond traditional investing.

- **[Chexy raises $14 million Series A led by Khosla Ventures](https://betakit.com/chexy-raises-14-million-series-a-led-by-khosla-ventures/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech startup Chexy has closed a $14 million Series A round led by Khosla Ventures, fuelling its growth in the rent payment and credit-building space.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a foundational moment for Canada's ongoing consumer-directed finance journey.

- **[Opendoor hiring in Canada as it looks to make Toronto a "major hub"](https://betakit.com/opendoor-hiring-in-canada-as-it-looks-to-make-toronto-a-major-hub/)** — *BetaKit* — US proptech and fintech platform Opendoor is expanding its Canadian footprint by actively hiring in Toronto as it aims to establish the city as a major operational hub.

- **[Federal minimum wage increasing to $18.15 per hour](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/federal-minimum-wage-increase-9.7140017?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's federal minimum wage is rising to $18.15 per hour, a development with direct implications for financial inclusion, household cash flow, and embedded payroll fintech solutions.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A provocative look at the darker side of fintech's rise, raising important questions about consumer protection, algorithmic bias, and financial exclusion that are highly relevant to Atlantic Canada's financial inclusion agenda.

- **[Towards an Open Banking API Standard](http://jackgavigan.com/2016/02/10/towards-an-open-banking-api-standard/)** — *Hacker News* — An early but foundational piece on developing open banking API standards, offering useful context as Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs prepare for Canada's incoming consumer-directed finance regime.
